title: Mastering EA FIFA FC 24: An In-Depth Guide to Skill Moves
content:
Originally published on Medium.

The thrill of outmaneuvering your opponent with a dazzling display of skill moves is one of the most exhilarating aspects of playing FIFA FC 24. Whether you’re aiming to break through a solid defense or simply add flair to your game, mastering skill moves can significantly enhance your gameplay experience. This guide provides a comprehensive list of FIFA FC 24 skill moves, categorized by their difficulty level, along with step-by-step instructions on how to execute them.

1-Star Skill Moves

  • First-Time Feint Turn: Hold L1 + R1 + flick LS down / Hold LB + RB + flick LS down
  • Bridge Skill: Double tap R1 / Double tap RB
  • Directional Nutmeg: Hold L1 + R1 + RS in any direction / Hold LB + RB + RS in any direction
  • Ball Juggle (while standing): L2 + tap R1 / LT + tap RB
  • Open Up Fake Shot Left: Hold L1 + Square or Circle then tap X + LS top left diagonally / Hold LB + X or B then tap A + LS top left diagonally
  • Open Up Fake Shot Right: Hold L1 + Square or Circle then tap X + LS top right diagonally / Hold LB + X or B then tap A + LS top right diagonally
  • Flick Up for volley: Hold R1 + click RS / Hold RB + click RS

2-Star Skill Moves

  • Feint Forward and Turn: Flick RS down twice / Flick RS down twice
  • Body Feint right/left: Flick RS right/left / Flick RS right/left
  • Stepover right/left: Roll RS from the top of the stick to the right/left / Roll RS from the top of the stick to the right/left
  • Reverse Stepover right/left: Roll RS from the right/left to the top of the stick / Roll RS from the right/left to the top of the stick
  • Ball Roll right/left: Hold RS right/left / Hold RS right/left

3-Star Skill Moves

  • Stutter Feint: Hold L2 + flick RS left then right (or right then left) / Hold LT + flick RS left then right (or right then left)
  • Heel Flick: Flick RS up and then down / Flick RS up and then down
  • Roulette right: Roll RS from the bottom and clockwise to the right / Roll RS from the bottom and clockwise to the right
  • Roulette left: Roll RS from the bottom and anti-clockwise to the left / Roll RS from the bottom and anti-clockwise to the left
  • Fake left and go right: Roll RS from the left anti-clockwise to the right / Roll RS from the left anti-clockwise to the right
  • Fake right and go left: Roll RS from the right clockwise to the left / Roll RS from the right clockwise to the left
  • Heel Chop right/left (while running): Hold L2 + Square or Circle then X + hold LS right/left / Hold LT + X or B then A + hold LS right/left
  • Heel to ball roll: Hold L1 + flick RS up then down / Hold LB + flick RS up then down
  • Ball Hop (while standing): Hold L1 + click RS / Hold LB + click RS
  • Heel to Heel Flick: Flick RS up and then down / Flick RS up and then down
  • Simple rainbow: Flick RS down, then up twice / Flick RS down, then up twice
  • Spin right: Hold R1 + roll RS from bottom clockwise to right / Hold RB + roll RS from bottom clockwise to right
  • Spin left: Hold R1 + roll RS from the bottom anti-clockwise to the left / Hold RB + roll RS from the bottom anti-clockwise to the left
  • Stop and Turn right/left (while running): Flick RS up then right/left / Flick RS up then right/left
  • Ball roll cut right: Hold RS left then hold LS right / Hold RS left then hold LS right
  • Ball roll cut left: Hold RS right then hold LS left / Hold RS right then hold LS left
  • Fake pass (while standing): Hold R2 + Square or Circle then X / Hold RT + X or B then A
  • Fake pass exit right/left (while standing): Hold R2 + Square or Circle then X + flick LS top right/left diagonally / Hold RT + X or B then A + flick LS top right/left diagonally
  • Quick ball rolls: Hold RS down / Hold RS down
  • Drag to Heel: Hold L1 + flick RS down, then right or left / Hold LB + flick RS down, then right or left
  • Lane Change right/left: Hold L1 + hold RS right/left / Hold LB + hold RS right/left
  • Three-touch roulette right/left: Hold L2 + flick RS down then right/left / Hold LT + flick RS down then right/left
  • Drag backspin right/left: Flick RS down then right/left / Flick RS down then right/left

5-Star Skill Moves

  • Elastico: Roll RS from right clockwise to left / Roll RS from right clockwise to left
  • Reverse Elastico: Roll RS from left anti-clockwise to right / Roll RS from left anti-clockwise to right
  • Advanced Rainbow: Flick RS down, hold up, then flick up / Flick RS down, hold up, then flick up
  • Hocus Pocus: Roll RS from bottom clockwise to left, then roll anti-clockwise to right / Roll RS from bottom clockwise to left, then roll anti-clockwise to right
  • Triple Elastico: Roll RS from bottom anti-clockwise to right, then roll clockwise to left / Roll RS from bottom anti-clockwise to right, then roll clockwise to left
  • Ball Roll and Flick Left (while running): Hold RS left then flick up / Hold RS left then flick up
  • Ball Roll and Flick Right (while running): Hold RS right then flick up / Hold RS right then flick up
  • Heel Flick Turn: Hold L2 + R1 + flick RS up then down / Hold LT + RB + flick RS up then down
  • Sombrero Flick (while standing): Flick RS up, up, down / Flick RS up, up, down
  • Turn and Spin Left: Flick RS up then left / Flick RS up then left
  • Turn and Spin Right: Flick RS up then right / Flick RS up then right
  • Ball Roll Fake Left (while standing): Hold RS left then flick RS right / Hold RS left then flick RS right
  • Ball Roll Fake Right (while standing): Hold RS right then flick RS left / Hold RS right then flick RS left
  • Ball Roll Fake Turn: Hold L2 + flick RS up then flick RS either left or right / Hold LT + flick RS up then flick RS either left or right
  • Rabona Fake (while jogging): Hold L2 + Square or Circle then X + LS down / Hold LT + X or B then A + LS down
  • Elastico Chop Left: Hold L2 + hold R1 then roll RS from right clockwise to left / Hold LT + hold RB then roll RS from right clockwise to left
  • Elastico Chop Right: Hold L2 + hold R1 then roll RS from left anti-clockwise to right / Hold LT + hold RB then roll RS from left anti-clockwise to right

title: Mastering the Two Pointers Technique: An In-Depth Guide
content:
Originally published on Medium.

The world of coding interviews is filled with a variety of patterns and techniques designed to solve common algorithmic problems efficiently. One such powerful and versatile technique is the Two Pointers approach. This article aims to provide an in-depth understanding of the Two Pointers technique, its applications, and how to implement it to tackle a range of problems.

What is the Two Pointers Technique?

The Two Pointers technique involves using two pointers (or indices) to traverse and process data structures, typically arrays or linked lists. The idea is to have one pointer start from the beginning of the structure and the other from the end (or another strategic position), and move them towards each other or in specific directions based on the problem’s requirements.

When to Use the Two Pointers Technique

The Two Pointers technique is particularly useful in scenarios such as:

  1. Finding Pairs or Triplets: When looking for pairs or triplets that meet certain conditions, such as summing up to a specific value.
  2. Sorting Problems: When merging sorted arrays or partitions.
  3. Palindrome Checking: To verify if a string reads the same backward as forward.
  4. Partitioning Arrays: To segregate elements based on certain criteria.

Example Problems and Solutions

Let’s explore some common problems where the Two Pointers technique shines.

Problem 1: Two Sum II — Input Array Is Sorted

Description: Given an array of integers numbers that is already sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number. Return the indices of the two numbers (1-indexed).

Solution:

def two_sum(numbers, target):
    left, right = 0, len(numbers) - 1
    
    while left < right:
        current_sum = numbers[left] + numbers[right]
        if current_sum == target:
            return [left + 1, right + 1]
        elif current_sum < target:
            left += 1
        else:
            right -= 1
    
    return []

# Example usage
numbers = [2, 7, 11, 15]
target = 9
print(two_sum(numbers, target))  # Output: [1, 2]

Explanation:

  • We initialize two pointers, left at the beginning and right at the end of the array.
  • We calculate the sum of the elements at these pointers.
  • If the sum equals the target, we return the indices.
  • If the sum is less than the target, we move the left pointer to the right.
  • If the sum is greater than the target, we move the right pointer to the left.

Problem 2: Container With Most Water

Description: Given n non-negative integers a1, a2, ..., an, where each represents a point at coordinate (i, ai). n vertical lines are drawn such that the two endpoints of line i is at (i, ai) and (i, 0). Find two lines, which together with the x-axis forms a container, such that the container contains the most water.

Solution:

def max_area(height):
    left, right = 0, len(height) - 1
    max_area = 0
    
    while left < right:
        width = right - left
        current_area = min(height[left], height[right]) * width
        max_area = max(max_area, current_area)
        
        if height[left] < height[right]:
            left += 1
        else:
            right -= 1
    
    return max_area

# Example usage
height = [1,8,6,2,5,4,8,3,7]
print(max_area(height))  # Output: 49
  • We initialize two pointers, left at the beginning and right at the end of the height array.
  • We calculate the area formed between the lines at these pointers and update max_area if the current area is greater.
  • We move the pointer pointing to the shorter line inward, aiming to find a taller line that could potentially form a larger area.

Problem 3: Valid Palindrome

Description: Given a string s, determine if it is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.

Solution:

def is_palindrome(s):
    left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
    
    while left < right:
        while left < right and not s[left].isalnum():
            left += 1
        while left < right and not s[right].isalnum():
            right -= 1
        if s[left].lower() != s[right].lower():
            return False
        left += 1
        right -= 1
    
    return True

# Example usage
s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
print(is_palindrome(s))  # Output: True

Explanation:

  • We initialize two pointers, left at the beginning and right at the end of the string.
  • We skip non-alphanumeric characters and compare the remaining characters.
  • If characters at left and right are not the same (ignoring cases), we return False.
  • We continue moving the pointers inward until they meet.

Conclusion

The Two Pointers technique is a powerful tool for solving a wide range of problems efficiently. By strategically moving two pointers, we can simplify complex tasks and achieve optimal solutions. Mastering this technique not only enhances problem-solving skills but also prepares you for various coding interviews and competitive programming challenges.

title: Multimodal AI: The Next Frontier in Personalized Education
content:
Originally published on Medium.

Introduction: OpenAI launched their new model, ChatGPT-4o, yesterday on May 13th. To showcase its capabilities, they shared a video of a student using the model to solve and understand a complex geometry problem. Watching this video brought back memories of my own struggles with geometry as a student. The AI’s ability to provide clear, step-by-step guidance was truly impressive. You can view the video here. This demonstration highlights how multimodal AI is transforming personalized education, making complex subjects more accessible and engaging for students.

The Power of Personalized Learning

Personalized learning tailors educational experiences to individual students’ needs, strengths, and interests. Traditional classroom settings often struggle to provide this level of customization, leading to a one-size-fits-all approach that can leave some students behind. AI-powered teaching assistants offer a solution by delivering personalized content and feedback, adapting to each student’s pace and style of learning.

ChatGPT-4o: A Leap Forward

ChatGPT-4o represents a significant leap in AI technology, primarily due to its multimodal processing capabilities. This means the model can understand and generate responses not just from text, but also from images, audio, and video inputs. This multimodality allows for richer, more engaging interactions that can cater to various learning styles.

Key Features and Benefits

  1. Multimodal Learning: With the ability to process multiple forms of input, ChatGPT-4 can provide explanations through text, diagrams, videos, and even audio. This versatility ensures that complex concepts are accessible to all students, regardless of their preferred learning medium.
  2. Real-Time Feedback: Immediate feedback is crucial for effective learning. ChatGPT-4o can assess student responses in real-time, offering instant corrections and suggestions, which helps reinforce learning and build confidence.
  3. Interactive Simulations: The model can guide students through interactive simulations, making abstract concepts tangible. For example, a student learning physics can engage with virtual experiments, observing the effects of changing variables in real-time.
  4. Accessibility: ChatGPT-4o’s multimodal capabilities also enhance accessibility for students with disabilities. For instance, visually impaired students can benefit from audio explanations, while those with hearing impairments can receive detailed visual content.

Real-World Applications

In practice, ChatGPT-4 can be integrated into various educational platforms and tools. One notable example is its integration with Khan Academy, where it serves as a virtual tutor, assisting students with homework, providing detailed explanations, and even offering encouragement and motivation.

Future Prospects

The future of personalized teaching with AI looks promising. As models like ChatGPT-4o continue to evolve, we can expect even more sophisticated interactions and personalized learning experiences. The potential to cater to each student’s unique needs will only grow, making education more inclusive and effective.

Conclusion

The advent of ChatGPT-4o and its multimodal capabilities marks a new era in personalized teaching assistance. By providing tailored, engaging, and accessible educational experiences, this technology is set to revolutionize how we learn. As we embrace these advancements, the future of education looks brighter than ever, with AI paving the way for a more personalized and effective learning journey for all.

title: On the Road to Autonomy: The Transformative Impact of Self-Driving Cars
content:
Originally published on Medium.

Introduction

Imagine a world where cars drive themselves, streets are safer, and traffic jams are a thing of the past. This isn’t just a futuristic vision; it’s quickly becoming a reality, and I’ve witnessed it firsthand. As a Tesla owner, I’ve experienced the evolution of Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology, currently at version 12.3.6. Launched initially in 2022, the early versions of FSD were admittedly rough around the edges — rides were bumpy, and the car made frequent errors in judgment. However, each update has brought significant improvements. The latest version is a testament to a major step change in autonomous technology, primarily driven by Tesla’s innovative approach of using imitation learning. The latest version is a testament to a major step change in autonomous technology, primarily driven by Tesla’s innovative use of pure vision-based imitation learning. This approach eschews traditional sensors and instead uses cameras to learn and improve from observing human driving behaviors, making it increasingly efficient and accurateToday, we are not just envisioning an autonomous future; we are actively driving towards it, with technologies that transform our transportation systems more comprehensively and rapidly than ever before.

Safety Improvements

One of the most compelling benefits of autonomous vehicles is the potential to drastically reduce accidents caused by human errors such as distraction, fatigue, or impaired driving. Using Vision and AI, AVs can detect obstacles, predict behaviors of pedestrians and other drivers, and make quick decisions that human reflexes may fail to make.

Economic Impact

Autonomous driving promises to reshape economies by transforming transportation costs. Logistics companies can cut major expenses with driverless trucks that run 24/7 without breaks, optimizing delivery times and fuel usage. Furthermore, the emergence of robo-taxis and on-demand car services could significantly decrease the need for private car ownership, altering the automotive industry’s business model.

Environmental Benefits

Most AVs are electric, aligning with global efforts to reduce carbon emissions. By optimizing driving patterns and reducing idle times, these vehicles can contribute to substantial decreases in urban smog and greenhouse gas emissions. Efficient driving not only benefits the planet but also conserves energy resources.

Societal Benefits

Autonomous vehicles can offer unprecedented mobility to those who need it most, including the elderly and individuals with disabilities, granting them independence and improving their quality of life. Additionally, city landscapes could transform with fewer cars on the roads, leading to less congestion and smaller or no parking lots, creating more space for public parks or housing.

Challenges and Considerations

Despite these benefits, autonomous driving also faces significant hurdles. The transition involves complex regulatory, legal, and ethical challenges. Legislation must evolve to address issues such as liability in accidents involving AVs and ensuring these vehicles can make ethical decisions in unavoidable crash scenarios.

Future Outlook

As technology progresses, we can expect AVs to become more integrated into our daily lives. However, the speed at which this integration takes place will depend on technological, regulatory, and societal readiness. Looking ahead, continuous advancements in AI and machine learning will further enhance the safety and efficiency of autonomous vehicles.

Conclusion

The road to a driverless future is paved with both incredible potential and formidable challenges. As we stand on the brink of this automotive revolution, it’s crucial to foster informed discussions, rigorous policy development, and proactive public engagement to steer the future of autonomous driving in the right direction.

title: Beyond Coding: The Rise of Product Engineers in the AI Era
content:
Originally published on Medium.

Introduction: Historically, software engineers spent considerable time on mundane coding tasks, areas where AI can now excel. For software engineers, this shift signals a transformative change in job roles, responsibilities, and skills required. This article delves into how AI is revolutionizing the software industry and underscores why new engineers must transition into product engineers to remain relevant and thrive in this evolving landscape.

The Impact of AI on Software Jobs

Artificial intelligence is increasingly automating traditional programming tasks. With tools like GitHub Copilot, which suggests code snippets and entire functions, the basic coding jobs are becoming less about writing every line of code and more about understanding and supervising the code that machines generate.

  1. Automation of Routine Tasks: Routine coding tasks are being automated away. This is both a challenge and an opportunity. Engineers need to shift their focus from mundane tasks to more complex problem-solving activities.
  2. Rise of Machine Learning and Data Science: As AI technologies evolve, there’s a growing demand for professionals skilled in machine learning, data science, and neural networks. Software engineers must gain expertise in these areas to design, implement, and manage AI-driven applications.
  3. Integration Across Disciplines: AI is not just a tool for tech companies. It’s becoming integral in fields like healthcare, finance, and even creative industries. This requires engineers to have a broader understanding of various domains where AI can be applied effectively.

The Shift to Product Engineering

The role of a software engineer is rapidly evolving into that of a product engineer. This shift is driven by the need to not only understand and use technology but also to integrate it seamlessly into user friendly products.

  1. User-Centric Approach: Engineers must adopt a user-centric approach to product development. This involves understanding user needs and behavior, which goes beyond traditional engineering skills.
  2. Cross-Functional Skills: Product engineers are expected to have cross-functional skills including project management, user experience design, and even marketing. Understanding the product lifecycle and being able to work collaboratively with diverse teams are essential skills.
  3. Innovative Mindset: In the age of AI, creativity and innovation are more crucial than ever. Engineers need to be able to think outside the box to develop solutions that leverage AI technologies in new and effective ways.

Preparing for the Future

For new engineers entering the field, the message is clear: the landscape is changing, and adaptability is key. Educational institutions and existing professionals must focus on cultivating the skills needed for these new roles.

  1. Continuous Learning: Staying updated with the latest technological advancements and continually upgrading skills will be essential. This might mean pursuing specialized courses in AI and machine learning or attending workshops and seminars on product development.
  2. Mentorship and Networking: Building a strong professional network and seeking mentorship can provide insights and guidance necessary for navigating this changing landscape.
  3. Embracing Soft Skills: In addition to technical prowess, soft skills such as communication, teamwork, and problem-solving are becoming indispensable. These skills enable engineers to effectively collaborate and lead within their organizations and with external stakeholders.
  4. Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Engineers should seek opportunities to collaborate across disciplines. This broadens their understanding and ability to innovate at the intersections of technology, business, and user experience.

Conclusion

The integration of artificial intelligence into software development is not just changing how products are built; it’s redefining the very nature of the software engineering role. As we step into this new era, embracing the role of product engineer — with a focus on innovation, user experience, and cross-disciplinary skills — will be crucial for the next generation of engineers.

Embrace the change, and let AI be your tool, not your replacement.

title: Coding Pattern I — Sliding Window
content:
Originally published on Medium.

In the realm of algorithmic problem-solving, the Sliding Window technique stands out for its efficiency and elegance, especially in handling array or list-related challenges. This approach is particularly useful when dealing with problems that require examining a subset of elements in a sequence. In this article, we’ll delve into the Sliding Window pattern, exploring its concept through five practical coding examples in Python.

The Sliding Window technique involves creating a ‘window’ over a portion of the data structure (like an array or a list) and then moving that window throughout the data to capture different subsets of elements. This method is highly effective for problems requiring the analysis of contiguous sequences of data within a larger set. Imagine you have a row of toys, and you’re only allowed to look at a few at a time through a cardboard tube (your “window”). You slide this tube along the row to see different sets of toys.

Example 1: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K

def max_sum_subarray_of_size_k(nums, k):
    max_sum, window_sum, window_start = 0, 0, 0
    for window_end in range(len(nums)):
        window_sum += nums[window_end]  # Add the next element
        # Slide the window, no need to slide if we've not hit the required window size of 'k'
        if window_end >= k - 1:
            max_sum = max(max_sum, window_sum)
            window_sum -= nums[window_start]  # Subtract the element going out
            window_start += 1  # Slide the window ahead
    return max_sum

The “sliding window” technique demonstrated in the code efficiently finds the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size `k` within an array `nums`. It initializes variables to track the current window’s sum (`window_sum`), the maximum sum found (`max_sum`), and the starting index of the current window (`window_start`). The algorithm iterates through `nums`, expanding the window by adding new elements to `window_sum` and checking if the window has reached the desired size `k`. If it has, the algorithm potentially updates `max_sum` with the current `window_sum`, then slides the window forward by one element, both by removing the element at the start of the window from `window_sum` and incrementing `window_start`. Finally, the algorithm returns `max_sum`, representing the largest sum found in any `k`-sized subarray within `nums`.

Example 2: Finding the longest substring with at most two distinct characters

def smallest_subarray_with_given_sum (s, arr):
    window_start, window_sum = 0, 0
    min_length = float('inf')
    
    for window_end in range(len(arr)):
        window_sum += arr[window_end]
        while window_sum > s and window_start <= window_end:
            window_sum -= arr[window_start]
            window_start += 1
        
          if window_sum == s:
            min_length = min(min_length, window_end - window_start + 1)
            window_sum -= arr[window_start]
            window_start += 1

    return min_length if min_length != float('inf') else 0

The code employs the “sliding window” technique to determine the length of the smallest subarray within an array `arr` whose sum exactly equals a specified value `s`. It initializes variables to keep track of the current window’s sum (`window_sum`), the smallest subarray length found (`min_length`), and the starting index of the current window (`window_start`). As the algorithm iterates through `arr`, it expands the window by adding elements to `window_sum`. If `window_sum` exceeds `s`, the algorithm shrinks the window from the beginning, updating `window_sum` by subtracting the outgoing elements, until `window_sum` is no longer greater than `s`. When `window_sum` equals `s`, the algorithm updates `min_length` with the size of the current window, if it’s smaller than previously found lengths, and then moves the window forward by one element to search for other potential subarrays. Ultimately, the algorithm returns `min_length`, which reflects the length of the smallest contiguous subarray summing to `s`, or 0 if no such subarray exists. This method is particularly useful in identifying the most compact sequence within an array that meets a precise sum requirement.

Example 3: Longest Substring with Maximum K Distinct Characters

def longest_substring_with_k_distinct(s,k):
    window_start, max_length = 0, 0
    char_index_map = {}
    
    for window_end in range(len(s)):
        right_char = s[window_end]
        char_index_map[right_char] = window_end
        
        if(len(char_index_map) > k):
            left_char_index = min(char_index_map.values())
            del char_index_map[s[left_char_index]]
            
            window_start = left_char_index + 1
            
        max_length = max(max_length, window_end - window_start + 1)
        
    return max_length

In this solution, we use the “sliding window” technique to identify the longest substring containing at most K distinct characters in a given string. We track each character’s latest position using a dictionary and adjust the window’s boundaries accordingly. If the window contains more than K distinct characters, we reduce it from the left, removing the earliest occurring character. This continues until the entire string is examined, ensuring the identification of the longest valid substring. This method is both efficient and straightforward, effectively addressing this typical string manipulation challenge.

title: Demystifying Authentication: Sessions, Tokens, JWT, SSO, and OAuth 2.0
content:
Originally published on Medium.

In the digital world, ensuring that a user is who they claim to be is crucial and ensuring secure access and interaction with applications is paramount. This requires understanding both authentication (verifying user identity) and authorization (granting access to resources). Today, we’ll explore key mechanisms in this landscape: Sessions, JWT (JSON Web Tokens), Token-based authentication, SSO (Single Sign-On), and OAuth 2.0, highlighting their roles in authentication and authorization.

1. Session-based Authentication (Authentication)

Session-based authentication is a traditional method where the server creates a session for the user after verifying their credentials (e.g., username and password). A session ID is then sent to the user’s browser, encapsulated in a cookie, marking the browser with a unique identifier for the duration of the session. This approach is straightforward but has limitations like the inability to fully control the login lifecycle and challenges in supporting mobile apps.

2. JWT (JSON Web Tokens) (Authentication/Authorization)

JWTs are a modern solution for secure communication between parties. A JWT is a compact token that consists of a header, a payload, and a signature. The server generates a JWT after authenticating the user and sends it to the client. The client then uses this token for subsequent requests. JWTs can serve both as an authentication mechanism (verifying the user’s identity) and an authorization tool (carrying claims about what resources the user is allowed to access).

3. Token-based Authentication (Authentication/Authorization)

Token-based authentication is a broad term that encompasses methods like JWT. It involves issuing a token (a small piece of data) to a user after successful authentication. This token is then used for authorizing future requests by the user, helping to manage sessions and access to resources. This method supports mobile applications effectively, overcoming the limitations of traditional session cookies.

4. SSO (Single Sign-On) (Authentication)

SSO is an authentication scheme that allows users to log in with a single ID and password to access a range of systems or applications. It’s primarily an authentication mechanism, streamlining the login process for users across multiple platforms. SSO systems often rely on a central authentication server that verifies user identities and then informs other applications about the user’s authenticated state.

5. OAuth 2.0 (Authorization)

OAuth 2.0 is a framework for authorization. It allows third-party applications to obtain limited access to an HTTP service, either on behalf of a resource owner by orchestrating an approval interaction between the resource owner and the HTTP service, or by allowing the third-party application to obtain access on its own behalf. This makes OAuth 2.0 strictly an authorization mechanism, differentiating it from authentication solutions like SSO.

6. Conclusion

The world of online security is complex, filled with different tools for keeping things safe and managing who gets access to what. By understanding how these tools — like session IDs, JWTs, tokens, single sign-on, and OAuth 2.0 — work, anyone making apps or websites can ensure they’re both easy to use and protected from unwanted access.

title: Simplifying API Client Integration: The Shift from REST to GraphQL
content:
Originally published on Medium.

API client integration with REST APIs presents challenges throughout the software development lifecycle, including inefficient data handling, scalability issues, and lengthy integration processes, negative developer experience. This article examines GraphQL as an effective REST alternative, emphasizing its streamlined approach to API integration, enhanced data retrieval efficiency, and benefits for scalability and developer productivity.

Understanding the Challenges of REST APIs

REST APIs have been the cornerstone of web services due to their straightforward, standardized approach. However, they come with a set of universal challenges:

  1. Inefficient Data Retrieval: The need to interact with multiple endpoints to collect necessary information leads to over-fetching and under-fetching, reducing performance and user satisfaction.
  2. Complex Integration Processes: The fixed structure of REST endpoints often results in cumbersome and time-consuming integration efforts, impeding development agility and innovation.
  3. Scalability Issues: Growing API ecosystems tend to exacerbate the complexity and scalability challenges, placing additional burdens on client applications and infrastructure.

The Advantages of Adopting GraphQL

The transition to GraphQL has been motivated by its promise to address the shortcomings of REST APIs. Here’s how GraphQL confronts these challenges:

1. Precise Data Fetching:

GraphQL empowers clients to request exactly what they need, no more, no less. This targeted approach eliminates unnecessary data exchange, optimizing performance and application efficiency.

2. Streamlined Integration:

A unified GraphQL endpoint simplifies the data retrieval process, reducing the complexity and time associated with integrating diverse API functionalities. This change significantly accelerates development workflows and simplifies client interactions.

3. Enhanced Scalability:

The adaptable nature of GraphQL facilitates smoother API evolution and better resource management, effectively mitigating the scalability concerns that plague REST APIs. This adaptability ensures that APIs can evolve without compromising existing client implementations.

Evaluating the Impact of the Transition

The adoption of GraphQL marks a significant paradigm shift in API development and integration, yielding tangible benefits:

  • Developer Efficiency: By enabling precise data queries, GraphQL reduces the burden on developers, allowing for more straightforward and effective API integrations.
  • Operational Improvement: Simplified data retrieval and reduced server load lead to improved maintenance and faster response times, enhancing overall operational efficiency.
  • Superior User Experiences: End users benefit from more responsive and efficient applications, thanks to the optimized data delivery mechanisms of GraphQL.

Case Study: How One Shopify Partner Increased Performance and Reliability

This case study from Shopify illustrates the transition from using REST APIs to GraphQL by Fisher Technology, a Shopify Plus Technology Partner. Fisher Technology faced challenges with the REST Admin API, such as hitting call limits and experiencing synchronization delays in their integrations. With the growth of their clients’ businesses, they needed a more efficient solution.

Rich Borek, Senior Business Process Consultant at Fisher Technology, recognized the limitations of REST APIs and explored the potential of GraphQL. By attending Shopify Unite 2018 and learning about GraphQL, Rich saw an opportunity to address the scalability issues they were facing.

Transitioning to the GraphQL Admin API enabled Fisher Technology to make more efficient API calls, reducing the number of calls required for their integrations. For example, they were able to sync data for 40,000 customers with only 40,000 API calls, compared to the previous 200,000 calls needed with REST APIs.

The adoption of GraphQL brought several benefits to Fisher Technology:
1. Improved performance: Integrations were faster, and customer databases could be synced more quickly between Shopify stores and ERP systems.
2. Increased agility: Fisher Technology could support larger clients and spend less time implementing workarounds to avoid call limits.
3. Resilience to changes: Using GraphQL made their integrations less susceptible to platform or API updates, as they could easily adapt their queries to retrieve only the necessary data.

Overall, transitioning to the GraphQL Admin API has had a positive impact on Fisher Technology’s business, allowing them to build more reliable integrations, improve client satisfaction, and increase their confidence in handling large volumes of data efficiently.

https://www.shopify.com/partners/blog/graphql-vs-rest

Conclusion: Embracing a New Standard in API Integration

The transition from REST to GraphQL represents more than a mere technological upgrade; it signifies a strategic move towards more efficient, flexible, and scalable client-server interactions. As the web development landscape continues to evolve, adopting GraphQL can provide a competitive edge, addressing the dynamic needs of modern applications and users.

title: Integrating ChatGPT into Your Daily Life: A Practical Guide
content:
Originally published on Medium.

In today’s fast-paced world, artificial intelligence (AI) has become more than just a buzzword. It’s transforming how we live, work, and interact. One of the most accessible forms of AI is ChatGPT, an advanced language model developed by OpenAI. This tool can revolutionize your daily routine, making tasks easier and providing instant access to a wealth of knowledge. In this post, I’ll guide you through practical ways to incorporate ChatGPT into your everyday life.

1. Personal Learning Assistant

Continuous Learning: Whether you’re a student, a lifelong learner, or someone looking to expand your knowledge, ChatGPT can be your go-to learning assistant. Struggling with a complex concept or need a quick explanation? ChatGPT can break down topics into understandable chunks, from quantum physics to historical events.

Language Practice: If you’re learning a new language, practice makes perfect. ChatGPT can converse with you in multiple languages, helping you practice conversation, grammar, and vocabulary.

2. Professional Assistant

Emails and Writing: Drafting emails or documents can be time-consuming. ChatGPT can help you generate ideas, write drafts, or even create whole articles. While it’s not a substitute for your unique voice, it can certainly provide a solid starting point or help you overcome writer’s block.

Research and Data Analysis: Need to do quick research or analyze data? ChatGPT can provide summaries of research papers, explain data analysis concepts, and even assist with coding problems.

3. Daily Planning and Productivity

Organizing Your Day: ChatGPT can help you create to-do lists, set reminders, or plan your day effectively. It can suggest time management techniques and provide advice on how to tackle procrastination.

Meal Planning and Recipes: Looking for meal inspiration? ChatGPT can suggest recipes based on your dietary preferences and pantry items. It can also help with meal planning and grocery lists.

4. Entertainment and Creative Writing

Storytelling and Creative Projects: Whether you’re writing a book, crafting a screenplay, or just looking for creative writing prompts, ChatGPT can be an invaluable partner. It can generate ideas, develop characters, and suggest plot twists.

Games and Quizzes: ChatGPT can be a fun companion, offering trivia, quizzes, and even role-playing games. It’s a great way to unwind and test your knowledge on various topics.

5. Personal Advisor

Health and Fitness: While ChatGPT is not a substitute for professional medical advice, it can provide general information on health and fitness topics, suggest workouts, or help you track your fitness journey.

Mental Well-being: ChatGPT can offer strategies for stress management, mindfulness exercises, and motivational quotes. It’s a supportive resource for mental well-being.

Conclusion

ChatGPT is a versatile tool that can enhance various aspects of your daily life. However, it’s important to remember that it’s an AI and should be used as a supplement to human judgment, especially for critical decisions. As you integrate ChatGPT into your routine, remain curious, and don’t hesitate to explore its vast capabilities. Embrace this AI assistant and discover how it can make your daily life more efficient, informed, and enjoyable.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Everyday Life: A Seamless Integration
content:
Originally published on Medium.

In the last decade, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has moved from the realm of science fiction to an integral part of our daily lives. Far from the sentient robots of Hollywood’s imagination, AI today is enhancing the efficiency, convenience, and quality of everyday activities. From the moment we wake up to the time we go to sleep, AI is there, quietly reshaping the landscape of our routines.

1. The Dawn of AI: Personal Assistants

The day begins with AI without most of us even realizing it. Personal assistants like Siri, Alexa, and Google Assistant have become our morning companions, helping us check the weather, play music, or read the news with just a voice command. These AI-driven assistants learn from our habits and preferences to deliver more personalized responses, showcasing how machine learning algorithms are becoming increasingly integrated into our daily routines.

2. Smart Homes: The Automated Abode

As we move through our day, AI continues to enhance our living spaces. Smart home devices like Nest or Ring use AI to learn our preferences for temperature and security, automating tasks to save energy and provide peace of mind. Lights, thermostats, and even refrigerators are now part of the IoT (Internet of Things), working together to create a more efficient and responsive home environment.

3. Navigating the World: AI on the Go

Even when we leave our homes, AI follows. Navigation apps like Google Maps use AI to analyze traffic data and suggest the quickest routes. Ride-sharing services like Uber and Lyft optimize their services using AI, calculating prices and matching you with drivers to minimize wait times. These everyday conveniences are powered by complex algorithms that make sense of vast amounts of data in real time.

4. Healthcare: AI as a Lifesaver**

Perhaps one of the most impactful applications of AI is in healthcare. Machine learning algorithms are being used to predict patient outcomes, personalize treatment plans, and even diagnose diseases at earlier stages. AI-powered apps can monitor health metrics in real time, providing doctors with vital information and even alerting users to potential health issues before they become serious. The integration of AI in healthcare promises not only to improve individual outcomes but also to revolutionize the entire medical industry.

5. The Invisible Hand: AI in the Background**

What’s most remarkable about AI is often its invisibility. AI algorithms optimize supply chains for our groceries, manage the power grids that light our homes, and filter the spam from our inboxes. They curate the newsfeeds on our social media and recommend the next show to binge-watch. In countless ways, AI streamlines processes and makes our lives smoother without us even noticing.

The future is here, and it is automated. The journey of AI is an ongoing one, with each day bringing new advancements and possibilities. As we look to the future, one thing is clear: AI is no longer just a technological marvel; it is a daily reality, enhancing and simplifying every aspect of our lives.
